sentence_reviews.text: Learned vectors place semantically or functionally related entities near one another in a continuous space.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: word embeddings, latent semantic indexing, word2vec, GloVe, word analogies, and contextual word embeddings (lines 639-646); AIMA contents.html: word representations, word embeddings, and pretrained contextual representations (lines 628-657).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources directly support vector word representations/embeddings; semantic/functional relatedness and continuous-space neighborhood wording are synthesized from the embedding context.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Linear algebra, Information theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Embeddings & distributed representations rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Linear algebra.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: Chapter 7 'Linear Algebra' and word embeddings (lines 206-259 and 639-646); AIMA contents.html: Appendix A.2 vectors/matrices/linear algebra and word representations/embeddings (lines 768-774 and 628-657).
sentence_reviews.notes: Embeddings as vectors and linear algebra background support the dependency; exact rationale wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Embeddings & distributed representations consumes, emits, or organizes information using Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: linear algebra vector spaces/matrices and word embeddings (lines 206-259 and 639-646); AIMA contents.html: vectors/matrices/linear algebra and word representations/embeddings (lines 768-774 and 628-657).
sentence_reviews.notes: The vector/matrix nature of embeddings is well supported, though the full tensor phrasing is synthesized.
